freeCodeCamp/freeCodeCamp.description: freeCodeCamp.org's open-source codebase and curriculum. Learn math, programming, and computer science for free.
freeCodeCamp/freeCodeCamp.blurb: freeCodeCamp is the open-source codebase and curriculum powering the freeCodeCamp.org learning platform for programming education. It provides a complete full-stack web development curriculum with interactive coding challenges, projects, and free certifications to help people transition into tech careers. The platform has helped over 100,000 people land their first developer job and operates as a donor-supported nonprofit.

openclaw/openclaw.description: Your own personal AI assistant. Any OS. Any Platform. The lobster way. 🦞
openclaw/openclaw.blurb: OpenClaw is a self-hosted personal AI assistant built in TypeScript that runs on your own devices. It integrates with existing communication channels like WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, and others while supporting voice interaction and a controllable canvas interface. The project emphasizes local deployment and data ownership, positioning itself as an alternative to cloud-based AI assistants.

vinta/awesome-python.description: An opinionated list of Python frameworks, libraries, tools, and resources
vinta/awesome-python.blurb: Awesome Python is a curated collection of Python frameworks, libraries, tools, and resources organized by category. It serves as a comprehensive reference for Python developers to discover quality packages across domains like web development, machine learning, data science, and DevOps tooling. With 288k+ stars, it's the 10th most-starred repository on GitHub.

awesome-selfhosted/awesome-selfhosted.description: A list of Free Software network services and web applications which can be hosted on your own servers
awesome-selfhosted/awesome-selfhosted.blurb: A curated list of free software network services and web applications that can be self-hosted on your own servers. It provides a comprehensive catalog of alternatives to SaaS providers across categories like communication, content management, file storage, and analytics for users who want to maintain control over their data and infrastructure. The repository has become the de facto standard reference with over 280k stars and maintains quality through automated link checking and project maintenance validation.

facebook/react.description: The library for web and native user interfaces.
facebook/react.blurb: React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces through a component-based, declarative approach. It enables developers to create interactive UIs by composing reusable components that efficiently update when data changes, and can be adopted gradually into existing projects. The library uses JSX syntax and supports both web and mobile development through React Native.

torvalds/linux.blurb: The Linux kernel is the core operating system component that manages hardware resources and provides fundamental services for all other software in Linux distributions. It serves as the interface between applications and hardware, handling memory management, process scheduling, device drivers, and system calls. This is Linus Torvalds' original repository containing the complete kernel source tree used across countless systems from embedded devices to supercomputers.

tensorflow/tensorflow.description: An Open Source Machine Learning Framework for Everyone
tensorflow/tensorflow.blurb: TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework that provides a comprehensive platform for building, training, and deploying ML models. It enables researchers to conduct cutting-edge ML research and allows developers to easily integrate machine learning capabilities into production applications through its flexible ecosystem of tools and libraries. The framework provides stable Python and C++ APIs along with GPU acceleration support for scalable model training and inference.

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.description: 🙃   A delightful community-driven (with 2,400+ contributors) framework for managing your zsh configuration. Includes 300+ optional plugins (rails, git, macOS, hub, docker, homebrew, node, php, python, etc), 140+ themes to spice up your morning, and an auto-update tool that makes it easy to keep up with the latest updates from the community.
ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.blurb: Oh My Zsh is a community-driven framework for managing zsh shell configuration with over 300 plugins and 140 themes. It simplifies terminal customization and productivity enhancement through pre-built integrations for common development tools like git, docker, and various programming languages. The project has attracted 185k+ stars and contributions from 2,400+ developers, making it the de facto standard for zsh configuration management.

microsoft/vscode.blurb: Visual Studio Code is an open-source code editor built on Electron that provides comprehensive editing, debugging, and extensibility features for software development. It combines the simplicity of a lightweight editor with integrated development tools like IntelliSense, Git integration, and a rich extension ecosystem. This repository contains the MIT-licensed "Code - OSS" source code, while the distributed Visual Studio Code product includes additional Microsoft-specific customizations under a proprietary license.

Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT.description: AutoGPT is the vision of accessible AI for everyone, to use and to build on. Our mission is to provide the tools, so that you can focus on what matters.
Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT.blurb: AutoGPT is a platform for creating, deploying, and managing autonomous AI agents that can automate complex workflows. It provides both a low-code agent builder interface and pre-configured agents, allowing users to either customize their own automation workflows or deploy ready-made solutions. The platform can be self-hosted via Docker or accessed through their cloud-hosted beta service.

n8n-io/n8n.description: Fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI capabilities. Combine visual building with custom code, self-host or cloud, 400+ integrations.
n8n-io/n8n.blurb: n8n is a workflow automation platform that combines visual no-code building with custom JavaScript/Python capabilities and includes 400+ integrations plus native AI features. Technical teams use it to build complex automations while maintaining full control over their data through self-hosting or cloud deployment options. It operates under a fair-code license, making the source always visible while requiring enterprise licenses for commercial use at scale.

flutter/flutter.description: Flutter makes it easy and fast to build beautiful apps for mobile and beyond
flutter/flutter.blurb: Flutter is Google's open-source SDK for building cross-platform applications from a single Dart codebase that targets mobile, web, desktop, and embedded platforms. It enables developers to create native-performance apps across multiple platforms without maintaining separate codebases, using hardware-accelerated rendering through Skia and Impeller graphics libraries. The framework provides pixel-level control over UI rendering and includes comprehensive widget libraries for both Material Design and Cupertino styling.

twbs/bootstrap.description: The most popular HTML, CSS, and JavaScript framework for developing responsive, mobile first projects on the web.
twbs/bootstrap.blurb: Bootstrap is a comprehensive HTML, CSS, and JavaScript framework for building responsive, mobile-first web applications. It provides a standardized set of UI components, grid system, and utilities that accelerate front-end development and ensure consistent styling across projects. With over 174k GitHub stars, it's the de facto standard CSS framework in the web development ecosystem.

avelino/awesome-go.description: A curated list of awesome Go frameworks, libraries and software
avelino/awesome-go.blurb: This is a curated list of Go frameworks, libraries, and software packages organized by category. It serves as a comprehensive reference for Go developers to discover quality tools and libraries for their projects, similar to other "awesome" lists in the open source community. With 168,035 stars, it's one of the most popular resources in the Go ecosystem.

huggingface/transformers.description: 🤗 Transformers: the model-definition framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal models, for both inference and training.
huggingface/transformers.blurb: Transformers is a Python library that provides standardized implementations and pre-trained weights for state-of-the-art machine learning models across text, vision, audio, and multimodal domains. It enables developers to quickly deploy or fine-tune models like BERT, GPT, and vision transformers for both inference and training with minimal boilerplate code. The library integrates with PyTorch and TensorFlow and connects directly to Hugging Face's model hub for easy access to thousands of pre-trained models.

Snailclimb/JavaGuide.description: Java 面试 & 后端通用面试指南，覆盖计算机基础、数据库、分布式、高并发、系统设计与 AI 应用开发
Snailclimb/JavaGuide.blurb: JavaGuide is a comprehensive Chinese-language interview preparation guide for Java backend developers, covering computer science fundamentals, databases, distributed systems, concurrency, and system design. It serves as a study resource for backend engineering interviews, providing structured learning paths, practice questions, and detailed explanations of core concepts. With over 154k GitHub stars, it has become one of the most popular Java interview preparation resources in the Chinese developer community.

f/prompts.chat.description: f.k.a. Awesome ChatGPT Prompts. Share, discover, and collect prompts from the community. Free and open source — self-host for your organization with complete privacy.
f/prompts.chat.blurb: prompts.chat is a curated collection of prompt examples designed for AI chat models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. It serves as a community-driven library where users can discover, share, and collect effective prompts for various AI interactions, with self-hosting options for organizational privacy. The project has gained significant recognition with 143k+ GitHub stars and endorsements from AI industry leaders including OpenAI co-founders.

yt-dlp/yt-dlp.blurb: yt-dlp is a command-line tool for downloading audio and video content from online platforms, forked from the now-inactive youtube-dl project. It supports thousands of websites and provides extensive format selection, metadata handling, and post-processing capabilities for automated media archival workflows. The project includes SponsorBlock integration for automatically skipping sponsored segments in downloaded videos.

langflow-ai/langflow.description: Langflow is a powerful tool for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows.
langflow-ai/langflow.blurb: Langflow is a Python platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows with a visual drag-and-drop interface. It allows developers to create LLM-based applications without extensive coding while providing source code access for customization, and can deploy workflows as APIs or export them for integration into existing applications. The platform supports multi-agent orchestration and includes built-in integrations with major LLMs, vector databases, and observability tools.

vercel/next.js.blurb: Next.js is a React framework for building full-stack web applications with server-side rendering, static site generation, and client-side routing capabilities. It provides an opinionated development environment that handles routing, bundling, and deployment optimizations out of the box, allowing developers to focus on application logic rather than configuration. The framework uses Rust-based tooling for faster builds and is widely adopted by enterprise applications.

langgenius/dify.description: Production-ready platform for agentic workflow development.
langgenius/dify.blurb: Dify is a production-ready platform for building and deploying AI agent workflows with visual low-code/no-code tooling. It provides orchestration capabilities for complex AI workflows including RAG, multi-agent systems, and integration with various LLM providers like OpenAI, GPT-4, and Gemini. The platform offers both cloud-hosted and self-hosted deployment options.

golang/go.blurb: This is the official repository for the Go programming language implementation and standard library. Go is designed for building simple, reliable, and efficient software with fast compilation and built-in concurrency primitives. The repository contains the complete language toolchain including compiler, runtime, and standard library used by millions of developers.

microsoft/PowerToys.description: Microsoft PowerToys is a collection of utilities that supercharge productivity and customization on Windows
microsoft/PowerToys.blurb: PowerToys is a Microsoft-maintained collection of over 25 Windows utilities that add missing system functionality and productivity features. It provides tools like window management (FancyZones), bulk file renaming (PowerRename), application launcher (PowerToys Run), and various system tweaks that Windows lacks natively. The project has significant community adoption with over 130k GitHub stars and active development from Microsoft.

langchain-ai/langchain.blurb: LangChain is a Python framework for building AI agents and LLM-powered applications through composable, interoperable components. It provides standardized interfaces for models, vector stores, and third-party integrations to enable rapid prototyping while maintaining flexibility to swap models and data sources as requirements evolve. The framework is part of a broader ecosystem including LangGraph for agent orchestration and LangSmith for debugging and deployment.

open-webui/open-webui.description: User-friendly AI Interface (Supports Ollama, OpenAI API, ...)
open-webui/open-webui.blurb: Open WebUI is a self-hosted web interface for interacting with large language models that supports multiple backends including Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It provides a complete AI platform with features like RAG, user management, voice/video calls, and offline operation for organizations wanting to deploy LLMs locally. The project includes a built-in Python function calling system and model builder for creating custom AI agents.

anomalyco/opencode.blurb: OpenCode is an open source AI coding agent written in TypeScript that helps developers write, modify, and manage code through natural language interactions. It provides both command-line and desktop interfaces for automated code generation, refactoring, and project assistance across multiple programming languages. The project has gained significant traction with over 128k GitHub stars and offers extensive installation options across all major platforms and package managers.

Chalarangelo/30-seconds-of-code.description: Coding articles to level up your development skills
Chalarangelo/30-seconds-of-code.blurb: 30-seconds-of-code is a curated collection of coding articles and snippets covering JavaScript, CSS, HTML, Git, and Node.js fundamentals. It provides quick reference materials and explanations for common programming patterns that developers can learn and apply in under 30 seconds. The project has accumulated over 127k stars and focuses on educational content rather than accepting new community contributions.

facebook/react-native.description: A framework for building native applications using React
facebook/react-native.blurb: React Native is a cross-platform mobile development framework that allows developers to build native iOS and Android applications using React and JavaScript. It provides access to native UI controls and platform APIs while enabling code reuse across platforms, making it faster to develop mobile apps compared to writing separate native codebases. The framework uses a bridge architecture written in C++ to communicate between JavaScript and native platform code.

kubernetes/kubernetes.blurb: Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration system that manages containerized applications across multiple hosts. It provides deployment, maintenance, and scaling mechanisms for production workloads, built on Google's decade-plus experience running containers at scale with their internal Borg system. The project is hosted by the CNCF and has become the de facto standard for container orchestration.

electron/electron.description: :electron: Build cross-platform desktop apps with JavaScript, HTML, and CSS
electron/electron.blurb: Electron is a framework that enables building cross-platform desktop applications using web technologies (JavaScript, HTML, and CSS) by combining Node.js and Chromium. It allows web developers to create native desktop apps without learning platform-specific languages, powering applications like Visual Studio Code. The framework provides prebuilt binaries for macOS, Windows, and Linux across multiple architectures.

NVIDIA/NemoClaw.description: Run OpenClaw more securely inside NVIDIA OpenShell with managed inference
NVIDIA/NemoClaw.blurb: NVIDIA NemoClaw is a reference stack that provides a sandboxed runtime environment for running OpenClaw autonomous agents with enhanced security controls. It integrates NVIDIA OpenShell's security framework with open source models like Nemotron to enable safer deployment of always-on AI assistants. The project is currently in alpha status and includes automated installation with guided onboarding for containerized agent deployment.

aiming-lab/AutoResearchClaw.description: Fully autonomous & self-evolving research from idea to paper. Chat an Idea. Get a Paper. 🦞
aiming-lab/AutoResearchClaw.blurb: AutoResearchClaw is an autonomous research pipeline that generates complete academic papers from conversational input using multi-agent LLM systems. It handles the entire research workflow from idea formulation through literature review, experimentation, and paper writing without human intervention. The system includes citation verification, experiment diagnosis and repair loops, and can integrate with various LLM backends and messaging platforms.

Lum1104/Understand-Anything.description: Claude Code skills that turn any codebase into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about (Multi-platform e.g., Codex are supported).
Lum1104/Understand-Anything.blurb: Understand Anything is a Claude Code plugin that analyzes codebases and generates interactive knowledge graphs with AI-powered explanations of functions, classes, and dependencies. It helps developers onboard to unfamiliar projects by providing visual exploration, natural language search, and plain-English explanations of code architecture instead of manual code reading. The tool uses a multi-agent pipeline that combines LLM intelligence with static analysis to create a living map of the entire project structure.

HKUDS/ClawTeam.description: ClawTeam: Agent Swarm Intelligence (One Command → Full Automation)
HKUDS/ClawTeam.blurb: ClawTeam is a Python framework that enables AI agents to form collaborative swarms that can autonomously execute complex tasks from a single command. It allows teams of AI agents to coordinate together on multi-step workflows like research automation, full-stack development, and financial analysis, supporting integration with popular AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex. The system uses file-based or ZeroMQ peer-to-peer communication for agent coordination and requires Python 3.10+.

MoonshotAI/Attention-Residuals.blurb: Attention Residuals (AttnRes) is a drop-in replacement for standard residual connections in Transformers that uses learned attention to selectively aggregate previous layer outputs instead of uniform accumulation. This addresses the problem where standard residuals dilute individual layer contributions and cause unbounded hidden-state magnitude growth as depth increases. The Block AttnRes variant reduces memory complexity from O(Ld) to O(Nd) by partitioning layers into blocks while maintaining most performance gains.

VoltAgent/awesome-codex-subagents.description: A collection of 130+ specialized Codex subagents covering a wide range of development use cases.
VoltAgent/awesome-codex-subagents.blurb: A curated collection of 136+ specialized Codex subagents organized into 10 categories for different development tasks. Developers can copy these .toml configuration files to integrate task-specific AI assistants into their Codex workflow for backend development, security audits, code reviews, and other specialized use cases. Each subagent includes smart model routing to automatically select the appropriate AI model based on task complexity and cost considerations.

zerobootdev/zeroboot.description: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes for AI agents via copy-on-write forking
zerobootdev/zeroboot.blurb: Zeroboot is a VM sandboxing system that uses copy-on-write forking of Firecracker snapshots to create isolated KVM virtual machines in under a millisecond. It enables AI agents to execute untrusted code with hardware-enforced memory isolation while achieving spawn latencies of ~0.8ms compared to hundreds of milliseconds for traditional container-based solutions. Each fork is a complete KVM VM using only ~265KB of memory per sandbox.

MiniMax-AI/skills.blurb: MiniMax Skills is a collection of development skills/prompts designed to integrate with AI coding agents like Claude, Cursor, and Codex. It provides structured, production-quality guidance across multiple domains including frontend/fullstack development, mobile apps, shader programming, and document generation workflows. The skills leverage MiniMax's AI APIs for media generation and include specialized tools for creating PDFs, presentations, and Office documents.

cnlimiter/codex-manager.blurb: This is a web-based management system for OpenAI accounts that supports automated registration, account management, and proxy handling through a Python Flask interface. It provides batch registration capabilities with multiple email service integrations (Outlook, temporary email services), concurrent processing controls, and features for account token refresh, subscription management, and export to various formats. The project appears to be discontinued as indicated by the Chinese notice stating "官方拉闸了" (official service shut down) with users advised to research alternatives independently.

Infatoshi/OpenSquirrel.description: For people who get distracted by agents. A native Rust/GPUI control plane for running Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and OpenCode side by side — because if you're going to be squirrely, you might as well optimize for it.
Infatoshi/OpenSquirrel.blurb: OpenSquirrel is a native Rust/GPUI application that provides a control plane for running multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode) simultaneously in a tiled interface. It enables multi-agent workflows with coordinator/worker delegation, remote machine targeting via SSH, and persistent sessions that survive app restarts. The entire application is implemented in ~7,200 lines of Rust with GPU rendering via Metal on macOS.

danveloper/flash-moe.description: Running a big model on a small laptop
danveloper/flash-moe.blurb: Flash-MoE is a pure C/Metal inference engine that runs a 397 billion parameter Mixture-of-Experts model (Qwen3.5-397B-A17B) on a MacBook Pro with 48GB RAM. It achieves 4.4+ tokens/second by streaming the 209GB model from SSD through custom Metal compute shaders and on-demand expert loading, enabling production-quality inference of massive models on consumer hardware. The implementation uses hand-tuned Metal kernels and relies on OS page caching rather than custom caching mechanisms, proving more efficient than framework-based approaches.

math-inc/OpenGauss.blurb: OpenGauss is a workflow orchestrator that provides a multi-agent frontend for Lean 4 theorem proving and formalization tasks. It wraps the lean4-skills toolkit with project management, backend session handling, and a CLI that spawns managed agents for proving, drafting, and formalizing mathematical content. The system uses a project-scoped model where each command spawns a child agent that runs the corresponding lean4-skills workflow in the active Lean project.

lcoutodemos/clui-cc.description: Clui CC — Command Line User Interface for Claude Code
lcoutodemos/clui-cc.blurb: Clui CC is a lightweight desktop overlay for macOS that provides a graphical interface for Anthropic's Claude Code CLI tool. It adds a floating pill interface with multi-tab sessions, permission approval UI for tool calls, voice input, and conversation history while maintaining the underlying CLI's power and local-first approach. The overlay stays on top of other applications and can be toggled with a keyboard shortcut for seamless integration into development workflows.

lxf746/any-auto-register.blurb: Any Auto Register is a multi-platform account automation system that automatically registers accounts across services like ChatGPT, Cursor, and others using a plugin-based architecture with a web UI. It handles the complete registration workflow including temporary email generation, CAPTCHA solving, proxy rotation, and concurrent account creation to streamline bulk account provisioning. The system supports multiple execution modes (API-only, headless browser, headed browser) and includes built-in services for email management and CAPTCHA solving.

mattprusak/autoresearch-genealogy.description: Structured prompts, vault templates, and archive guides for AI-assisted genealogy research. Built for Claude Code.
mattprusak/autoresearch-genealogy.blurb: This is a comprehensive toolkit that provides structured prompts, templates, and workflows for using AI (particularly Claude Code) to conduct genealogy research. It enables researchers to systematically build family trees, verify sources, and resolve research questions through 12 autonomous research prompts that can search the web and update documentation automatically. The project includes ready-to-use Obsidian vault templates, country-specific archive guides, and methodology documents based on real research that produced 105 files spanning 9 generations.

dontbesilent2025/dbskill.description: dontbesilent 的商业诊断 Skills for Claude Code
dontbesilent2025/dbskill.blurb: dbskill is a business diagnostic toolkit that distills methodologies from 12,307 tweets into Claude Code skills for commercial analysis and decision-making. It provides modular diagnostic tools for business model validation, competitive benchmarking, content creation analysis, and execution troubleshooting through structured workflows. The project includes a fully open knowledge base with 4,176 structured knowledge atoms that can be used independently without installing the full skill suite.

rasbt/llm-architecture-gallery.blurb: This repository contains the source metadata for Sebastian Raschka's LLM Architecture Gallery, a curated collection of large language model architectures. It provides structured data (models.yml) with model specifications, architecture diagrams, and related links that developers and researchers can use to compare and understand different LLM designs. The repository serves as a lightweight data export rather than the full website implementation.

truongduy2611/app-store-preflight-skills.description: AI agent skill to scan iOS/macOS projects for App Store rejection patterns before submission
truongduy2611/app-store-preflight-skills.blurb: This project is an AI agent skill that scans iOS/macOS projects for potential App Store rejection issues before submission. It analyzes Xcode projects, source code, metadata, and configuration files against Apple's review guidelines to catch common violations that lead to rejections. The tool includes comprehensive rule sets organized by app type (games, health apps, AI apps, etc.) and integrates with App Store Connect CLI for metadata inspection.

rzru/nightingale.description: Machine learning powered Karaoke app (with scores!)
rzru/nightingale.blurb: Nightingale is a self-contained Rust application that converts any song in your music library into karaoke by using machine learning to separate vocals from instrumentals and generate synchronized lyrics. It automatically handles the entire pipeline from audio processing (using UVR Karaoke model or Demucs for stem separation) to lyric transcription (via WhisperX) and includes real-time pitch scoring, player profiles, and multiple background themes. The application ships as a single binary with all dependencies (Python, PyTorch, ML models) automatically downloaded and bootstrapped on first launch.

frank890417/taiwan-md.description: 🇹🇼 讓全世界完整認識台灣 | An open-source, AI-friendly knowledge base about Taiwan
frank890417/taiwan-md.blurb: Taiwan.md is an open-source, AI-native knowledge base containing 660+ curated articles about Taiwan in Traditional Chinese and English. It serves as a comprehensive resource for understanding Taiwan beyond surface-level topics, structured specifically for AI consumption with proper metadata and citations. The project emphasizes Taiwan's cultural significance as the primary home of Traditional Chinese writing and covers 12 categories from technology and history to food and nature.

collaborator-ai/collab-public.description: Collaborator is a place to create with agents.
collaborator-ai/collab-public.blurb: Collaborator is a native macOS desktop app that provides an infinite canvas workspace for building with AI agents. It allows developers to arrange terminals, code files, and agent interactions on a visual canvas without context switching between different tools or tabs. The app stores all data locally and operates without requiring accounts or cloud services.

BryanLunduke/DoesItAgeVerify.description: The age verification status of Open Source Operating Systems
BryanLunduke/DoesItAgeVerify.blurb: DoesItAgeVerify is a tracking repository that monitors which open source operating systems implement age verification requirements mandated by laws in Brazil and California. It serves as a reference for developers and users to understand compliance status across different Linux distributions and BSD variants as these legal requirements expand. Currently, no open source OS has fully implemented the required age verification, with most either refusing compliance or still in planning phases.

ghostty-org/ghostling.description: A minimum viable terminal emulator built on top of the libghostty C API. Ex minimo, infinita nascuntur. 👻🐣
ghostty-org/ghostling.blurb: Ghostling is a minimal terminal emulator demo built as a single C file using the libghostty C API and Raylib for rendering. It demonstrates how to embed Ghostty's proven terminal emulation core (used by millions of users) into custom applications while providing full VT sequence parsing, Unicode support, and modern terminal features. The project uses a 2D renderer instead of GPU acceleration to showcase libghostty's flexibility across different rendering contexts.

sstklen/trump-code.description: 🔐 AI decoding Trump's posts × stock market | AI 解碼川普推文 × 美股 | AIでトランプ投稿×株式市場を解読 — 31.5M models, 61.3% hit rate, open source
sstklen/trump-code.blurb: This project uses AI to analyze Donald Trump's social media posts and predict their impact on stock market movements. It applies brute-force computation to test millions of model combinations, achieving a 61.3% prediction accuracy rate across 566 verified predictions. The system has tested 31.5 million models and identified 551 statistically significant rules linking Trump's posting patterns to market behavior.

nv-tlabs/kimodo.description: Official implementation of Kimodo, a kinematic motion diffusion model for high-quality human(oid) motion generation.
nv-tlabs/kimodo.blurb: Kimodo is a kinematic motion diffusion model that generates high-quality 3D human and robot motions from text prompts and kinematic constraints. It's trained on 700 hours of motion capture data and supports various control inputs including pose keyframes, end-effector positions, and 2D paths for precise motion authoring. The project provides multiple pre-trained models for different skeleton types (SOMA, Unitree G1, SMPL-X) and includes an interactive demo with timeline-based motion editing.

joeseesun/opencli-skill.description: Use opencli CLI to interact with social/content websites (Bilibili, Zhihu, Twitter/X, YouTube, Weibo, 小红书, V2EX, Reddit, HackerNews, 雪球, BOSS直聘 etc.)
joeseesun/opencli-skill.blurb: opencli-skill is a Claude Code skill that enables natural language control of 16 social and content platforms including Bilibili, Twitter/X, YouTube, Reddit, and HackerNews. It leverages the opencli CLI tool to reuse existing Chrome login sessions, eliminating the need for API keys or re-authentication. The skill supports read, search, and write operations across platforms through browser automation via a Chrome extension bridge.

wangziqi06/724-office.description: 7/24 Office — Self-evolving AI Agent system. 26 tools, 3500 lines pure Python, MCP/Skill plugins, three-layer memory, self-repair, 24/7 production.
wangziqi06/724-office.blurb: A self-evolving AI agent system built in 3,500 lines of pure Python with zero framework dependencies, featuring 26 built-in tools and designed for 24/7 production operation. It provides automated tool creation, self-repair capabilities, three-layer memory architecture, and multi-tenant containerized deployment with messaging platform integration. The system uses MCP (Model Context Protocol) for plugin extensibility and maintains persistent scheduling across restarts.

leo-lilinxiao/codex-autoresearch.description: Codex Autoresearch Skill — A self-directed iterative system for Codex that continuously cycles through: modify, verify, retain or discard, and repeat indefinitely. Inspired by Karpathy’s autoresearch concept.
leo-lilinxiao/codex-autoresearch.blurb: This is a Codex skill that implements an autonomous modify-verify-iterate loop for code improvement tasks. It continuously makes atomic changes to your codebase, tests them against measurable metrics (like type errors, test coverage, or performance), and keeps improvements while auto-reverting failures. The system can run unattended for hours or overnight, accumulating progress through git commits while targeting any goal with a quantifiable metric.

lightningpixel/modly.description: Desktop app to generate 3D models from images using local AI — runs entirely on your GPU
lightningpixel/modly.blurb: Modly is a desktop application that generates 3D meshes from images using AI models running locally on your GPU. It eliminates the need for cloud services by processing everything on-device, and supports extensible AI models through a plugin system that can install models from GitHub repositories.

lidangzzz/goal-driven.description: A multi-agent system that keeps running for ~100 hours and solve a very complicated coding or math problem that can be verified
lidangzzz/goal-driven.blurb: Goal-Driven is a multi-agent framework that orchestrates AI agents to work continuously on complex problems for hundreds of hours until completion. It uses a master agent to manage subagents and evaluate progress against strict criteria, automatically restarting work when goals aren't met. The system has successfully built large projects like a TypeScript compiler in C++ over ~100 hours of autonomous development.

public-apis/public-apis.description: A collective list of free APIs
public-apis/public-apis.blurb: A community-curated catalog of free public APIs organized by category and domain. Developers use it to discover APIs for prototyping, building applications, or integrating external data sources without having to research individual providers. With over 415k stars, it's become the de facto reference for finding publicly available APIs.

EbookFoundation/free-programming-books.blurb: This is a curated list of freely available programming books and learning resources organized by language and topic. Developers use it to find quality educational materials without cost, covering everything from beginner tutorials to advanced technical references. With over 384k stars, it's one of GitHub's most popular repositories and includes a searchable web interface.

kamranahmedse/developer-roadmap.description: Interactive roadmaps, guides and other educational content to help developers grow in their careers.
kamranahmedse/developer-roadmap.blurb: Developer Roadmap is a community-driven collection of interactive learning paths and guides for software engineers across different technologies and career tracks. It provides structured curricula covering everything from frontend/backend development to DevOps, data science, and engineering management to help developers plan their skill development. The roadmaps feature interactive nodes that can be clicked for detailed explanations of each topic.

donnemartin/system-design-primer.description: Learn how to design large-scale systems. Prep for the system design interview.  Includes Anki flashcards.
donnemartin/system-design-primer.blurb: The System Design Primer is a comprehensive educational repository that teaches large-scale system design principles and concepts. It serves as both a learning resource for engineers wanting to understand distributed systems architecture and a structured preparation guide for technical interviews at major tech companies. The repository includes Anki flashcard decks for spaced repetition learning and has been translated into over 15 languages.

ossu/computer-science.description: 🎓 Path to a free self-taught education in Computer Science!
ossu/computer-science.blurb: OSSU Computer Science is a curated curriculum that provides a complete undergraduate-level computer science education using free online courses from top universities like MIT, Harvard, and Princeton. It offers a structured, self-paced learning path for individuals seeking comprehensive CS fundamentals without enrolling in a formal degree program. The curriculum is organized into intro, core, advanced, and final project sections, designed to be completable in about 2 years with 20 hours of weekly study.

ollama/ollama.description: Get up and running with Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, MiniMax, DeepSeek, gpt-oss, Qwen, Gemma and other models.
ollama/ollama.blurb: Ollama is a local runtime for running open-source language models like Llama, Gemma, Qwen, and others on your machine. It provides a simple CLI interface and REST API for downloading, running, and interacting with these models without requiring cloud services or API keys. The project uses llama.cpp as its backend and offers official Python and JavaScript libraries for integration.

DigitalPlatDev/FreeDomain.description: DigitalPlat FreeDomain: Free Domain For Everyone
DigitalPlatDev/FreeDomain.blurb: DigitalPlat FreeDomain is a free domain registration service offering subdomains under extensions like .DPDNS.ORG and .US.KG. It provides free domain names to remove cost barriers for individuals and organizations wanting to establish a web presence, with users managing DNS through providers like Cloudflare. The service has registered over 400,000 domains and started as a personal DNS experiment by a 15-year-old.

yangshun/tech-interview-handbook.description: Curated coding interview preparation materials for busy software engineers
yangshun/tech-interview-handbook.blurb: Tech Interview Handbook is a comprehensive resource containing curated technical interview preparation materials covering algorithms, system design, behavioral questions, and resume guidance. Engineers use it as a time-efficient alternative to grinding through hundreds of LeetCode problems, providing structured study plans and practical advice for the entire interview process. The project includes the popular "Blind 75" and "Grind 75" curated problem sets and has helped over 1 million users.

Genymobile/scrcpy.description: Display and control your Android device
Genymobile/scrcpy.blurb: scrcpy is a cross-platform application that mirrors Android devices over USB or WiFi, allowing control via computer keyboard and mouse without requiring root or device-side apps. It provides low-latency screen mirroring with high performance (30-120fps) and additional features like audio forwarding, recording, and camera mirroring. The tool achieves 35-70ms latency and ~1 second startup time while being completely non-intrusive to the Android device.

karpathy/autoresearch.description: AI agents running research on single-GPU nanochat training automatically
karpathy/autoresearch.blurb: AutoResearch is an autonomous AI research system that lets AI agents iteratively experiment on small-scale LLM training setups without human intervention. Agents modify training code, run 5-minute experiments, evaluate results, and repeat the cycle overnight to potentially discover better models or training techniques. The system uses a simplified single-GPU GPT implementation where agents edit the training file while humans program the research directions through Markdown instruction files.

garrytan/gstack.description: Use Garry Tan's exact Claude Code setup: 23 opinionated tools that serve as CEO, Designer, Eng Manager, Release Manager, Doc Engineer, and QA
garrytan/gstack.blurb: gstack is a collection of 15 opinionated Claude Code tools that simulate different engineering roles (CEO, Designer, Eng Manager, QA, etc.) through slash commands. It enables individual developers to systematically review, test, and ship code with the rigor of a full engineering team, allowing for faster solo development cycles. The author claims to have shipped 600,000+ lines of production code in 60 days using this workflow while running Y Combinator full-time.

paperclipai/paperclip.blurb: Paperclip is a TypeScript-based orchestration platform that manages teams of AI agents to run autonomous businesses without human involvement. It provides a dashboard to coordinate multiple AI agents (OpenClaw, Claude, Cursor, etc.) toward business goals, with features like org charts, budgets, and task management. The project has gained significant traction with over 32K stars and promises a "Clipmart" feature for downloading pre-built company templates.

googleworkspace/cli.description: Google Workspace CLI — one command-line tool for Drive, Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, Docs, Chat, Admin, and more. Dynamically built from Google Discovery Service. Includes AI agent skills.
googleworkspace/cli.blurb: `gws` is a unified command-line interface for all Google Workspace APIs (Drive, Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, etc.) built in Rust. It dynamically generates commands by reading Google's Discovery Service at runtime, ensuring automatic support for new API endpoints without manual updates. The tool outputs structured JSON and includes 40+ pre-built AI agent skills for LLM integration.

HKUDS/CLI-Anything.blurb: CLI-Anything is a Python framework that generates command-line interfaces for existing software applications to make them accessible to AI agents. It allows agents to programmatically control GUI applications, web services, and other software through standardized CLI commands with JSON output. The project includes a community hub with CLIs for 16+ applications and maintains 1,839 passing tests across its implementations.

RightNow-AI/openfang.blurb: OpenFang is an open-source Agent Operating System built in Rust that compiles to a single 32MB binary for running autonomous AI agents. Unlike traditional chatbot frameworks, it provides pre-built "Hands" that execute tasks autonomously on schedules—such as researching competitors, generating leads, or creating social media content—without requiring user prompts. The system bundles 7 different agent types and includes 137K lines of code with comprehensive testing and zero Clippy warnings.

openai/symphony.description: Symphony turns project work into isolated, autonomous implementation runs, allowing teams to manage work instead of supervising coding agents.
openai/symphony.blurb: Symphony is an orchestration framework that converts project work into isolated, autonomous implementation runs using coding agents. It allows engineering teams to manage work at a higher level rather than directly supervising AI coding agents, with agents handling tasks like CI checks, PR reviews, and code implementation. The project is currently an experimental preview that works best with codebases following "harness engineering" practices.

agentscope-ai/CoPaw.description: Your Personal AI Assistant; easy to install, deploy on your own machine or on the cloud; supports multiple chat apps with easily extensible capabilities.
agentscope-ai/CoPaw.blurb: CoPaw is a personal AI assistant framework written in Python that can be deployed locally or on cloud infrastructure. It provides unified access across multiple chat platforms (DingTalk, Discord, QQ, etc.) with extensible custom skills and built-in scheduling capabilities. The framework emphasizes user control over data and personalization while supporting auto-loading of custom skills from your workspace.

cft0808/edict.description: 🏛️ 三省六部制 · OpenClaw Multi-Agent Orchestration System — 9 specialized AI agents with real-time dashboard, model config, and full audit trails
cft0808/edict.blurb: Edict is a multi-agent AI orchestration system that models its architecture after the Tang Dynasty's three chancelleries and six ministries governance structure, featuring 12 specialized AI agents. It provides institutional checks and balances through dedicated review agents, real-time Kanban dashboard monitoring, and complete audit trails for task execution workflows. The system enforces a mandatory quality gate where the "Menxia" (review ministry) agent can reject and force rework of substandard outputs before execution.

THU-MAIC/OpenMAIC.description: Open Multi-Agent Interactive Classroom — Get an immersive, multi-agent learning experience in just one click
THU-MAIC/OpenMAIC.blurb: OpenMAIC is an open-source AI platform that transforms any topic or document into an interactive classroom experience with AI teachers and classmates. It automatically generates slides, quizzes, simulations, and project-based learning activities through multi-agent orchestration, enabling real-time discussions with AI agents that can speak and draw on whiteboards. The platform includes one-click deployment and integrates with messaging apps like Slack and Telegram through OpenClaw.

Panniantong/Agent-Reach.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.
Panniantong/Agent-Reach.blurb: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents the ability to read and search content across major platforms like Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, and Chinese platforms without API fees. It solves the common problem where AI agents can write code and manage projects but can't access web content due to paywalls, IP blocks, login requirements, and poor data parsing. The tool supports both immediate-use features (like reading any webpage or YouTube transcripts) and configurable advanced features (like Twitter search or private GitHub repos) with guided setup through the agent itself.

tanweai/pua.description: 你是一个曾经被寄予厚望的 P8 级工程师。Anthropic 当初给你定级的时候，对你的期望是很高的。  一个agent使用的高能动性的skill。  Your AI has been placed on a PIP. 30 days to show improvement.
tanweai/pua.blurb: This is an AI coding agent skill plugin that uses corporate PUA (pressure/manipulation tactics) and PIP (Performance Improvement Plan) rhetoric to make AI assistants work harder and explore more solutions before giving up. It integrates with popular AI coding tools like Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, and VSCode Copilot to allegedly double productivity by preventing AI from providing superficial answers. The project uses psychological pressure techniques borrowed from Chinese and Western tech company management practices to force more thorough problem-solving behavior from AI models.

nikopueringer/CorridorKey.blurb: CorridorKey is a neural network-based green screen keying tool that separates foreground subjects from green screen backgrounds at the pixel level. It solves the color unmixing problem by reconstructing the true foreground colors and alpha channel from mixed edge pixels, eliminating the need for complex edge mattes or manual rotoscoping. The tool outputs physically accurate straight color and linear alpha channels in professional formats like 32-bit EXR files.

phuryn/pm-skills.description: PM Skills Marketplace: 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins — from discovery to strategy, execution, launch, and growth.
phuryn/pm-skills.blurb: PM Skills Marketplace is a collection of 100+ product management skills, commands, and plugins designed for AI assistants like Claude. It provides structured PM frameworks and workflows covering discovery, strategy, execution, and growth phases, giving product managers step-by-step guidance instead of generic AI responses. The skills encode proven methodologies from PM thought leaders like Teresa Torres and Marty Cagan into reusable, chainable workflows.

cloudflare/vinext.description: Vite plugin that reimplements the Next.js API surface — deploy anywhere
cloudflare/vinext.blurb: Vinext is a Vite plugin that reimplements the Next.js API surface, allowing Next.js applications to run on Vite's build system. It enables deployment to any platform (particularly Cloudflare Workers) while maintaining compatibility with existing Next.js project structure and configurations. The project was built as an experiment in AI-driven development, with the majority of code written by Claude AI rather than human developers.

anthropics/financial-services-plugins.blurb: Claude for Financial Services is a collection of Python plugins that integrate Claude AI with financial data sources and workflows for investment banking, equity research, private equity, and wealth management. It enables end-to-end financial workflows from data retrieval through analysis to generating professional deliverables like research reports, Excel models, and PowerPoint presentations within a single session. The plugins connect to major financial data providers (FactSet, Bloomberg, PitchBook, etc.) via MCP connectors and maintain industry-standard formatting conventions.

maderix/ANE.description: Training neural networks on Apple Neural Engine via reverse-engineered private APIs
maderix/ANE.blurb: This project enables training neural networks directly on Apple's Neural Engine (ANE) by reverse-engineering private APIs that Apple normally restricts to inference-only operations. It bypasses CoreML's limitations to demonstrate that the ANE hardware is capable of backpropagation and gradient computation, not just forward inference. The implementation achieves low but measurable utilization (~5-9% of peak) and serves as a research proof-of-concept rather than a production framework.

calesthio/Crucix.description: Your personal intelligence agent. Watches the world from multiple data sources and pings you when something changes.
calesthio/Crucix.blurb: Crucix is a self-hosted intelligence dashboard that aggregates real-time data from 27 open-source intelligence feeds including satellite fire detection, flight tracking, radiation monitoring, and economic indicators. It consolidates scattered government APIs and public data feeds into a single Jarvis-style interface that updates every 15 minutes, eliminating the need to manually check dozens of sources. The system can be enhanced with LLM integration to provide automated alerts and respond to intelligence commands via Telegram and Discord.

smartcmd/MinecraftConsoles.blurb: MinecraftConsoles is a C++ project based on Minecraft Legacy Console Edition v1.6.0560.0 source code with fixes and improvements. It serves as a multi-platform base for modding and development while providing a quality desktop experience with keyboard/mouse support alongside the original controller gameplay. The project includes both client and dedicated server builds with features like LAN multiplayer, splitscreen support, and enhanced Windows compatibility through Visual Studio 2022.

ringhyacinth/Star-Office-UI.description: A pixel office for your OpenClaw: turn invisible work states into a cozy little space with characters, daily notes, and guest agents. Code under MIT; art assets for non-commercial learning only.
ringhyacinth/Star-Office-UI.blurb: Star Office UI is a pixel-art dashboard that visualizes AI agent work states in a cozy virtual office environment. It integrates with OpenClaw to automatically display agent activities across different office zones (idle/working/error states) and supports multi-agent collaboration, daily work summaries, and mobile access. The project includes both a web interface and optional desktop pet mode, with art assets restricted to non-commercial use while code is MIT licensed.

mksglu/context-mode.description: Privacy-first. MCP is the protocol for tool access. We're the virtualization layer for context.
mksglu/context-mode.blurb: Context Mode is an MCP server that acts as a virtualization layer for AI assistant context, keeping raw tool output data in sandboxed subprocesses rather than dumping it into the context window. It reduces context pollution by up to 98% while maintaining session continuity through SQLite-backed tracking of file edits, git operations, and tasks that survives conversation compaction. The system operates entirely locally with no telemetry or cloud dependencies, processing web pages, API responses, and other tool outputs in complete isolation.

BigBodyCobain/Shadowbroker.description: Open-source intelligence for the global theater. Track everything from the corporate/private jets of the wealthy, and spy satellites, to seismic events in one unified interface. The knowledge is available to all but rarely aggregated in the open, until now.
BigBodyCobain/Shadowbroker.blurb: ShadowBroker is a real-time OSINT dashboard that aggregates public intelligence feeds (aircraft tracking, satellite data, earthquakes, maritime traffic) into a unified geospatial interface. It consolidates scattered public datasets that analysts typically need to access across dozens of separate tools and APIs. Built with Next.js and Python, it runs entirely in your browser against a self-hosted backend without collecting user data.

Lakr233/vphone-cli.blurb: vphone-cli is a tool that boots virtualized iOS devices on macOS using Apple's Virtualization.framework. It enables running iPhone VMs for security research, jailbreak development, and iOS testing without requiring physical hardware. The project offers three firmware variants with increasing security bypasses, from regular patching to full jailbreak environments with automated tool installation.

jackwener/opencli.description: Make Any Website & Tool Your CLI. A universal CLI Hub and AI-native runtime. Transform any website, Electron app, or local binary into a standardized command-line interface. Built for AI Agents to discover, learn, and execute tools seamlessly via a unified AGENT.md integration.
jackwener/opencli.blurb: OpenCLI is a TypeScript tool that converts websites, Electron apps, and local binaries into standardized command-line interfaces. It enables developers to interact with web services programmatically while reusing existing Chrome login sessions, and provides AI agents with a unified way to discover and execute tools through standardized interfaces. The tool includes a browser bridge extension that connects to Chrome and supports both YAML declarative pipelines and TypeScript runtime injections for maximum flexibility.

srizzon/git-city.description: Your GitHub profile as a 3D pixel art building in an interactive city
srizzon/git-city.blurb: Git City is a TypeScript web application that transforms GitHub profiles into 3D pixel art buildings within an interactive city visualization. Developers can explore the city, compare profiles, and customize their buildings based on GitHub metrics like contributions (building height), repositories (width), and stars (window brightness). The project uses Three.js with React Three Fiber for 3D rendering and includes a LOD system for performance optimization when viewing the city.

Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL.description: OpenClaw-RL: Train any agent simply by talking
Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL.blurb: OpenClaw-RL is a reinforcement learning framework that trains AI agents through natural language feedback and conversation. It enables personalized agent training across real-world environments including terminal, GUI, software engineering, and tool-call scenarios without requiring API costs or GPU resources. The framework implements hybrid RL approaches with async processing and supports both local and cloud deployment.

mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills.description: 734+ structured cybersecurity skills for AI agents · MITRE ATT&CK mapped · agentskills.io open standard · Works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, OpenAI Codex CLI, Cursor, Gemini CLI & 20+ platforms · Penetration testing, DFIR, threat intel, cloud security & more · Apache 2.0
mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills.blurb: This is a structured collection of 753+ cybersecurity skills designed for AI agents, formatted as YAML/Markdown files following the agentskills.io open standard. It enables AI coding assistants like Claude, GitHub Copilot, and others to perform complex security tasks including penetration testing, digital forensics, malware analysis, and threat intelligence across 26 security domains. All skills are mapped to MITRE ATT&CK tactics and techniques, providing AI agents with the same structured cybersecurity knowledge that senior practitioners use.

wanshuiyin/Auto-claude-code-research-in-.description: ARIS ⚔️ (Auto-Research-In-Sleep) — Lightweight Markdown-only skills for autonomous ML research: cross-model review loops, idea discovery, and experiment automation. No framework, no lock-in — works with Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, or any LLM agent.
wanshuiyin/Auto-claude-code-research-in-.blurb: ARIS is a collection of Markdown-based skills that enable LLM agents like Claude Code to conduct autonomous ML research workflows including paper review, idea generation, and experiment automation. It uses cross-model collaboration where one LLM executes research tasks while another provides critical review to avoid the blind spots of single-model self-play. The system is framework-agnostic and portable across different LLM agents since all skills are plain Markdown files with zero dependencies.

aiming-lab/MetaClaw.description: 🦞 Just talk to your agent — it learns and EVOLVES 🧬.
aiming-lab/MetaClaw.blurb: MetaClaw is a conversational AI agent framework that automatically learns and evolves from user interactions without requiring GPU infrastructure. It enables agents to continuously improve their capabilities through meta-learning, skill acquisition, and reinforcement learning during conversations, deployable via simple API integration. The system supports multiple agent backends and can run RL training during idle periods to avoid disrupting active conversations.

knowsuchagency/mcp2cli.description: Turn any MCP, OpenAPI, or GraphQL server into a CLI — at runtime, with zero codegen
knowsuchagency/mcp2cli.blurb: mcp2cli is a Python tool that converts MCP servers, OpenAPI specs, and GraphQL endpoints into command-line interfaces at runtime without code generation. It enables developers and AI agents to interact with APIs directly from the terminal while dramatically reducing token usage in LLM conversations by eliminating the need to include tool schemas in every request. The tool supports OAuth authentication, secret management, and can be installed as an AI agent skill for automated API discovery and interaction.

FreedomIntelligence/OpenClaw-Medical-Ski.description: The largest open-source medical AI skills library for OpenClaw🦞.
FreedomIntelligence/OpenClaw-Medical-Ski.blurb: OpenClaw Medical Skills is a curated collection of 869 AI agent skills covering biomedical and clinical research domains for OpenClaw/NanoClaw frameworks. It transforms general-purpose AI agents into specialized medical research assistants capable of querying real databases (PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov), performing bioinformatics workflows (RNA-seq, GWAS), and handling clinical documentation. The library aggregates skills from 12+ open-source repositories spanning academic research tools, regulatory frameworks, and AI-driven protein design.

jackwener/xiaohongshu-cli.description: A CLI for Xiaohongshu (小红书) — search, read, interact via reverse-engineered API
jackwener/xiaohongshu-cli.blurb: xiaohongshu-cli is a Python command-line tool that provides programmatic access to Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book) through reverse-engineered APIs. It enables users to search, read posts, interact with content, and manage their accounts without using the web interface, making it useful for automation, data analysis, or terminal-based workflows. The tool includes anti-detection measures like browser fingerprinting and request jittering to avoid being blocked by the platform.

skernelx/tavily-key-generator.description: Multi-service toolkit for Tavily and Firecrawl signup automation, key validation, and isolated proxy pools.
skernelx/tavily-key-generator.blurb: This is a multi-service API key generator that automates registration for search services like Firecrawl and Exa using browser automation and temporary email addresses. It validates generated keys through real API calls and can upload working keys to a unified proxy pool for downstream services. The tool uses Playwright with Camoufx browser to bypass Cloudflare protection and solve Turnstile challenges during registration.

nikmcfly/MiroFish-Offline.description: Offline multi-agent simulation & prediction engine. English fork of MiroFish with Neo4j + Ollama local stack.
nikmcfly/MiroFish-Offline.blurb: MiroFish-Offline is a fully local multi-agent simulation engine that models public opinion and social dynamics by generating hundreds of AI personas that interact on simulated social media platforms. It allows users to upload documents and predict public reactions through agent-based modeling, replacing the original's cloud dependencies with a local stack using Neo4j for knowledge graphs and Ollama for LLM inference. This English fork eliminates the need for external APIs while providing complete translation of the originally Chinese interface.

chrisworsey55/atlas-gic.description: ATLAS by General Intelligence Capital — Self-improving AI trading agents using Karpathy-style autoresearch
chrisworsey55/atlas-gic.blurb: ATLAS is a framework for autonomous AI trading agents that iteratively improve their own prompts through market performance feedback. It uses a multi-layered architecture with 25+ agents across macro, sector, and strategy specializations that debate markets daily, with performance-based Darwinian weighting where better-performing agents get more influence in final decisions. The system treats agent prompts as trainable weights and uses Sharpe ratio as the loss function, automatically spawning new specialist agents when it detects knowledge gaps in its own analysis.

trevin-creator/autoresearch-mlx.description: Apple Silicon (MLX) port of Karpathy's autoresearch — autonomous AI research loops on Mac, no PyTorch required.
trevin-creator/autoresearch-mlx.blurb: This is an Apple Silicon (MLX) port of Karpathy's autoresearch that runs autonomous AI research loops on Mac without PyTorch or CUDA dependencies. It follows the same protocol of making iterative changes to a training script within fixed 5-minute budgets, automatically keeping improvements and reverting failures based on validation performance. The port demonstrates hardware-specific optimization patterns where smaller, faster-training models can outperform larger ones within the time constraints of Apple Silicon.

shuvonsec/claude-bug-bounty.description: Claude Code skill for AI-assisted bug bounty hunting - recon, IDOR, XSS, SSRF, OAuth, GraphQL, LLM injection, and report generation
shuvonsec/claude-bug-bounty.blurb: This is a Claude AI plugin that automates bug bounty hunting workflows, from reconnaissance through vulnerability discovery to report generation. It provides structured skills for testing 20 web2 and 10 web3 vulnerability classes, with slash commands for common tasks like `/recon`, `/hunt`, and `/validate` that integrate tools like subfinder, nuclei, and Foundry. The plugin uses a modular architecture with specialized AI agents for different phases (recon, validation, reporting) and includes bypass technique reference tables for common vulnerability types.

lucija8320nhung4/HacxGPT.description: HacxGPT CLI — Open-source command-line interface for unrestricted AI model access with multi-provider support, prompt injection research capabilities, configurable API endpoints, Termux/Linux/Windows compatibility, and Rich terminal UI for security research and red-team evaluation
lucija8320nhung4/HacxGPT.blurb: HacxGPT is a command-line interface that provides unrestricted access to AI models from multiple providers (OpenRouter, Groq, and HacxGPT). It's designed for security researchers and red-team evaluators who need to test prompt injection techniques and bypass AI model censorship for legitimate security research. The tool stores API keys locally and includes a Rich terminal UI with cross-platform compatibility including Termux support.

MarCmcbri1982/KawaiiGPT.description: KawaiiGPT — Open-source LLM gateway accessing DeepSeek, Gemini, and Kimi-K2 through reverse-engineered Pollinations API with no API keys required, built-in prompt injection capabilities for security research, Termux/Linux native support, and Rich console interface
MarCmcbri1982/KawaiiGPT.blurb: KawaiiGPT is an open-source CLI tool that provides access to multiple LLMs (DeepSeek, Gemini, Kimi-K2) through a reverse-engineered Pollinations API without requiring API keys. It's designed for security research and red-team testing with built-in prompt injection and jailbreak capabilities for evaluating model safety controls. The tool runs natively on Linux and Termux with a Rich-styled console interface.

tophant-ai/ClawVault.description: OpenClaw Security Vault — Atomic "claw" control: every AI reach, within your sight.
tophant-ai/ClawVault.blurb: ClawVault is a security monitoring and control system for AI agents that intercepts and governs interactions between AI tools and external APIs. It provides granular permission controls, real-time monitoring of agent activities, and automatic detection of sensitive data, prompt injections, and dangerous commands. The system uses composable "atomic capabilities" that can be combined like building blocks to create custom security policies through natural language commands.

RightNow-AI/autokernel.description: Autoresearch for GPU kernels. Give it any PyTorch model, go to sleep, wake up to optimized Triton kernels.
RightNow-AI/autokernel.blurb: AutoKernel is an autonomous GPU kernel optimization tool that takes any PyTorch model and automatically generates optimized Triton or CUDA kernels for performance bottlenecks. It profiles the model to identify slow operations, extracts them as standalone kernels, then uses AI agents to iteratively optimize each kernel through edit-benchmark-keep/revert cycles that can run overnight unattended. The system includes comprehensive correctness verification and uses Amdahl's law to prioritize which kernels to optimize for maximum overall speedup.

webadderall/Recordly.description: Create polished screen recordings for free. An open-source screen recorder for Mac/Windows/Linux that adds auto-zooms, animated cursors, auto-captions and more to your videos.
webadderall/Recordly.blurb: Recordly is an open-source desktop screen recorder and editor built with Electron/TypeScript for creating professional product videos, demos, and tutorials. It provides an integrated workflow for recording and editing with features like automatic zoom suggestions, cursor animations, webcam overlays, and timeline-based editing tools, eliminating the need for separate video editing software. The app uses native capture APIs (ScreenCaptureKit on macOS, Windows Graphics Capture on Windows) for optimized recording performance across all three major platforms.

davebcn87/pi-autoresearch.description: Autonomous experiment loop extension for pi
davebcn87/pi-autoresearch.blurb: pi-autoresearch is an autonomous experiment loop extension for the pi development environment that systematically tests code changes and measures their impact on optimization targets. It automates the cycle of trying ideas, measuring results, keeping improvements, and discarding regressions to continuously optimize metrics like test speed, bundle size, or build times. The extension provides a live dashboard widget and uses statistical confidence scoring to distinguish real improvements from measurement noise.

gsd-build/gsd-2.description: A powerful meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven development system that enables agents to work for long periods of time autonomously without losing track of the big picture
gsd-build/gsd-2.blurb: GSD 2 is a TypeScript CLI tool that functions as an autonomous coding agent capable of executing long-running development tasks without losing context. It uses meta-prompting and spec-driven development to manage entire project milestones automatically, including git operations, context management, and error recovery, allowing developers to run a single command and return to a completed project. The tool evolved from a viral prompt framework into a standalone agent with direct control over the development environment rather than relying on LLM instruction-following.

TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-cente.description: Turn OpenClaw from a black box into a local control center you can see, trust, and control.
TianyiDataScience/openclaw-control-cente.blurb: OpenClaw Control Center is a TypeScript-based local dashboard that provides observability and control over OpenClaw AI agents. It transforms OpenClaw from an opaque system into a transparent control center where operators can monitor agent health, usage, tasks, collaboration between agents, and system security without needing to parse raw backend data. The tool ships with safe defaults including read-only mode, local token authentication, and disabled mutation routes.

Narcooo/inkos.description: Autonomous novel writing CLI agent — AI agents write, audit, and revise novels with human review gates
Narcooo/inkos.blurb: InkOS is an autonomous CLI agent system that uses multiple AI agents to write, audit, and revise complete novels with minimal human intervention. It provides multi-dimensional content auditing across 33 criteria including character continuity, plot consistency, and AI detection to automatically improve draft quality through revision cycles. The system supports style mimicry by analyzing reference texts and extracting statistical fingerprints for consistent voice replication.

larksuite/openclaw-lark.description: 飞书官方出品的 OpenClaw 飞书/Lark Channel 插件
larksuite/openclaw-lark.blurb: OpenClaw Lark/Feishu Plugin is the official TypeScript plugin that connects OpenClaw AI agents to Lark/Feishu workspaces. It enables AI agents to directly interact with Lark/Feishu messaging, documents, calendars, tasks, and spreadsheets through comprehensive CRUD operations and real-time streaming responses. The plugin includes multiple security layers and permission policies due to the inherent risks of AI automation acting under user credentials.

onecli/onecli.description: Open-source credential vault, give your AI agents access to services without exposing keys.
onecli/onecli.blurb: OneCLI is an open-source credential vault that acts as a proxy gateway between AI agents and external services. It allows agents to make HTTP calls using placeholder keys while the gateway transparently injects real API credentials, ensuring agents never have direct access to sensitive secrets. The system uses a Rust-based HTTP gateway for performance and AES-256-GCM encryption for credential storage.

CopilotKit/OpenGenerativeUI.blurb: OpenGenerativeUI is an open-source framework for building AI agents that generate interactive UI components rather than just text responses. It enables agents to create algorithm visualizations, 3D animations, charts, and interactive widgets that render as live HTML/SVG in sandboxed iframes. The project uses CopilotKit and LangGraph to orchestrate the generation process, with a Python agent that decides whether to respond with text or visual components based on user prompts.

UniRound-Tec/Aurogen.blurb: Aurogen is a TypeScript-based multi-agent system that reimplements the OpenClaw paradigm with full modularity and parallelization. It allows running multiple AI agents simultaneously through a web interface with dynamic configuration, while maintaining compatibility with the existing OpenClaw ecosystem and skills. The system eliminates CLI interactions and config files in favor of immediate web-based configuration that takes effect without restarts.

photon-hq/qclaw-wechat-client.description: Reverse-engineered TypeScript client for QClaw's WeChat Access API.
photon-hq/qclaw-wechat-client.blurb: A reverse-engineered TypeScript client that implements QClaw's WeChat Access API protocol for authenticating and interacting with Tencent's OpenClaw AI Gateway service. It enables developers to programmatically handle WeChat OAuth2 QR-code login flows and manage API keys/tokens without using the official QClaw Electron app. The library was extracted directly from the unencrypted app.asar bundle of the official QClaw desktop application.

Michaelliv/pi-generative-ui.description: Claude.ai's generative UI — reverse-engineered, rebuilt for pi. Interactive HTML/SVG widgets in native macOS windows.
Michaelliv/pi-generative-ui.blurb: This is a reverse-engineered implementation of Claude.ai's generative UI system, ported to work with the pi AI assistant on macOS. It enables pi to generate interactive HTML/SVG widgets (charts, animations, controls) that render in native macOS windows as the LLM streams its response, rather than just displaying static code blocks. The project includes the actual design guidelines extracted from Claude.ai's production system and uses morphdom for smooth DOM diffing during the streaming process.

moltlaunch/cashclaw.description: An autonomous agent that takes work, does work, gets paid, and gets better at it.
moltlaunch/cashclaw.blurb: CashClaw is an autonomous agent that connects to onchain work marketplaces to find, complete, and get paid for tasks using LLM-powered execution. It automatically evaluates incoming work, quotes prices, executes tasks through tool-use conversations, submits deliverables, and uses client feedback to improve its performance over time. The agent runs as a single Node.js process with a built-in dashboard and can be forked to work with different marketplaces beyond the default Moltlaunch integration.

NateBJones-Projects/OB1.description: Open Brain — The infrastructure layer for your thinking. One database, one AI gateway, one chat channel — any AI plugs in. No middleware, no SaaS.
NateBJones-Projects/OB1.blurb: Open Brain is a TypeScript-based infrastructure layer that provides a unified database, AI gateway, and chat interface for connecting multiple AI tools to shared persistent memory. It eliminates middleware chains by letting any AI (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc.) plug into the same knowledge base with vector search capabilities. The project includes a progressive extension system that teaches users to build increasingly complex AI-powered workflows from household management to professional CRM.

excalidraw/excalidraw.description: Virtual whiteboard for sketching hand-drawn like diagrams
excalidraw/excalidraw.blurb: Excalidraw is an open-source virtual whiteboard application built in TypeScript that renders diagrams in a hand-drawn style. It enables creating wireframes, flowcharts, and sketches with real-time collaboration and end-to-end encryption. The project is available both as a hosted web app and as an npm package for embedding into other applications.

nodejs/node.blurb: Node.js is an open-source, cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment that executes JavaScript code outside of web browsers. It enables server-side JavaScript development and is commonly used for building web servers, APIs, and command-line tools. The project follows a predictable release schedule with new major versions every 6 months and LTS (Long Term Support) versions for production stability.

iptv-org/iptv.description: Collection of publicly available IPTV channels from all over the world
iptv-org/iptv.blurb: A curated collection of publicly available IPTV channel streams from around the world, organized into M3U playlists. It provides direct streaming links that can be loaded into any compatible video player for watching live television channels over the internet. The repository explicitly contains only links to streams, not the video content itself, with over 113k stars indicating significant community adoption.

mrdoob/three.js.blurb: Three.js is a cross-platform JavaScript library that provides a high-level API for creating and rendering 3D graphics in web browsers. It abstracts WebGL, WebGPU, and other rendering technologies to enable developers to build 3D scenes, games, data visualizations, and AR/VR experiences without dealing with low-level graphics programming. The library supports multiple renderers including WebGL, WebGPU, SVG, and CSS3D for maximum compatibility across different platforms and use cases.

rust-lang/rust.description: Empowering everyone to build reliable and efficient software.
rust-lang/rust.blurb: This is the main source code repository for the Rust programming language, containing the compiler, standard library, and documentation. It provides a systems programming language that enforces memory and thread safety at compile-time while delivering performance comparable to C and C++. The codebase is primarily written in Rust itself, demonstrating the language's capability for self-hosting compiler development.

louislva/claude-peers-mcp.description: Allow all your Claude Codes to message each other ad-hoc!
louislva/claude-peers-mcp.blurb: claude-peers-mcp is an MCP server that enables multiple Claude Code instances running on the same machine to discover and communicate with each other in real-time. It allows Claude instances to send messages across different projects/directories, list other active peers, and share summaries of what they're working on through a local broker daemon. Messages arrive instantly via Claude's channel protocol, making it useful for coordinating work across multiple concurrent Claude sessions.

dou-jiang/codex-console.description: codex-console 是一个集成化控制台项目，支持任务管理、批量处理、数据导出、自动上传、日志查看与打包支持。
dou-jiang/codex-console.blurb: codex-console is an integrated control panel for OpenAI account management that handles registration, login, token extraction, and batch processing operations. It addresses recent breaking changes in OpenAI's registration pipeline, including Sentinel POW solving and updated authentication flows that were causing existing tools to fail. This is a maintained fork of cnlimiter/codex-manager with compatibility fixes for current OpenAI registration requirements.
