It reveals the internal architecture, tool system, telemetry collection, feature flags, and controversial capabilities like "undercover mode" that hides AI authorship in open-source contributions. The repository contains 40+ tools and exposes remote control mechanisms including killswitches that Anthropic can trigger to modify or disable functionality.

Kuberwastaken/claude-code.description: Claude Code in Rust & a Breakdown of How it Works
Kuberwastaken/claude-code.blurb: Claude Code is a reverse-engineered repository containing the leaked source code of Anthropic's official AI coding CLI tool, extracted from sourcemap files accidentally published to npm. Developers use this to examine the internal architecture and implementation details of Claude's coding assistant that were never meant to be public. The leak occurred because Anthropic forgot to exclude sourcemap files from their npm package, exposing 785KB of source code including system prompts and internal tooling.

chatgptprojects/claude-code.blurb: This is an unofficial repository containing the extracted TypeScript source code of Anthropic's Claude Code CLI tool, obtained by unpacking the source map from the official npm package. It provides access to the complete, unobfuscated codebase of Claude's terminal interface for software engineering tasks like file editing, command execution, and git workflows. The source was inadvertently exposed through a source map file that included the full `sourcesContent` in the published npm package.

tvytlx/claude-code-deep-dive.description: Claude Code 源码深度研究报告
tvytlx/claude-code-deep-dive.blurb: Claude Code Deep Dive is a reverse-engineering analysis of Anthropic's `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` npm package, extracting and documenting the complete source code from the CLI's source maps. The project provides a systematic study of Claude Code's architecture, including its prompt assembly system, agent orchestration, tool execution pipeline, and plugin ecosystem to understand why it outperforms typical LLM wrapper tools. The analysis reveals 4,756 extracted source files and positions Claude Code as an "Agent Operating System" rather than a simple CLI wrapper.

oboard/claude-code-rev.blurb: This is a reverse-engineered reconstruction of Claude Code's source tree, recovered primarily from source maps with missing modules replaced by compatibility shims. It enables developers to run a local development environment of Claude Code that was previously unavailable as open source. The restoration includes working CLI bootstrapping, skill documentation, and MCP compatibility layers, though some functionality remains degraded due to unrecoverable private integrations.

claude-code-best/claude-code.description: 原汁原昧 Claude Code 可运行,可构建版; Typescript 类型全修复; 企业级可靠性; 安全无毒, lock 文件保真, 可直接 bun i; bun run dev 启动
claude-code-best/claude-code.blurb: This is a reverse-engineered TypeScript implementation of Anthropic's official Claude Code CLI tool, recreated from decompiled source code. It provides a full REPL interface with streaming conversations, tool execution capabilities (file operations, bash commands, web search, etc.), and support for multiple API backends including Anthropic Direct, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex. The project explicitly warns about its uncertain longevity and recommends downloading rather than forking due to rapid updates driven by Claude Opus optimization.

obra/superpowers.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
obra/superpowers.blurb: Superpowers is an agentic skills framework that provides a structured software development workflow for coding agents through composable skills and initial instructions. It enforces a disciplined process where agents gather requirements, create validated designs, generate detailed implementation plans, and execute work through subagent-driven development rather than jumping straight into coding. The framework supports multiple AI coding platforms including Claude Code, Cursor, and others through plugin marketplaces or manual installation.

Leonxlnx/claude-code-system-prompts.description: Research into how agentic AI coding assistants work — reconstructed prompt patterns, agent coordination, and security classification
Leonxlnx/claude-code-system-prompts.blurb: This repository documents reconstructed system prompts and architectural patterns used by agentic AI coding assistants like Claude Code, based on behavioral analysis rather than leaked source code. It provides educational insights into how these systems coordinate multiple specialized agents, manage security classifications for tool usage, and dynamically assemble modular prompt architectures at runtime. The project catalogs 20+ specific prompt patterns covering everything from multi-agent orchestration to context window management and autonomous tool execution safety.

shipany-ai/open-agent-sdk.description: Agent-SDK without CLI dependencies, as an alternative to claude-agent-sdk, completely open source
shipany-ai/open-agent-sdk.blurb: Open Agent SDK is an open-source TypeScript library for building autonomous AI agents that can read code, edit files, run commands, and execute multi-step workflows. It provides an in-process alternative to Anthropic's official Claude Agent SDK, eliminating CLI dependencies and enabling deployment to cloud servers, serverless functions, and containers. The SDK supports custom tools, MCP server integration, and both one-shot queries and persistent multi-turn conversations.

hitmux/HitCC.description: Complete reverse-engineering documentation of the full logic of Claude Code CLI Node.js version v2.1.84
hitmux/HitCC.blurb: HitCC is a comprehensive reverse-engineering documentation project that analyzes the complete logic of Claude Code CLI Node.js version v2.1.84. It provides structured documentation of the CLI's execution chain, agent loop, tool systems, and surrounding ecosystem to serve as a reference for building alternative implementations or rewrites. The project contains 81 documentation files totaling over 27,000 lines covering everything from startup sequences to MCP integration and session persistence.

adamlyttleapps/claude-skill-aso-appstore.blurb: A Claude Code skill that automatically generates App Store screenshots by analyzing iOS app codebases to identify key benefits and pairing them with simulator screenshots. It uses a two-stage pipeline where deterministic Python scaffolding creates consistent layouts, then AI enhancement via Gemini MCP adds visual polish and photorealistic device frames. The tool outputs App Store-ready screenshots at exact Apple dimensions along with working files organized in a structured directory.

tvytlx/ai-agent-deep-dive.description: AI Agent 源码深度研究报告
tvytlx/ai-agent-deep-dive.blurb: This is a research repository containing a deep dive analysis of Claude Code's source code and architecture, focusing on how modern coding agents are built. The analysis examines system prompts, agent orchestration, tools, plugins, hooks, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to understand why these agents are more stable and effective than simple LLM+tool combinations. The repository provides a comprehensive PDF report but notably excludes the actual source code, focusing instead on educational analysis and commentary.

chatgptprojects/clear-code.description: See your claude code logs in clear details in your dashboard
chatgptprojects/clear-code.blurb: Clear-Code is a curated repository that catalogs and compares open-source AI coding assistants like Cline, Aider, and Continue. It helps developers find alternatives to proprietary tools like GitHub Copilot and Cursor without vendor lock-in, privacy concerns, or enterprise licensing costs. The project focuses specifically on open-source solutions that can be self-hosted and customized.

NanmiCoder/claude-code-haha.description: Claude Code leaked source - locally runnable version
NanmiCoder/claude-code-haha.blurb: This is a locally runnable version of Claude Code based on leaked source code, with fixes to make the Ink TUI interface functional. It allows users to run the official Claude Code interface locally while supporting custom Anthropic-compatible APIs like MiniMax and OpenRouter. The project includes both interactive TUI mode and headless operation for scripting scenarios.

ponponon/claude_code_src.description: 2026.3.31 claude code 意外把包含源码的文件上传到 npm 仓库，版本号是 2.1.88，其中 cli.js.map 文件有 57MB 的体积，claude code 的源码在该文件的 sourcesContent 字段里面，解压还原后有 70w 行代码
ponponon/claude_code_src.blurb: This repository contains the recovered source code of Anthropic's Claude Code CLI tool version 2.1.88, extracted from a sourcemap file that was accidentally published to npm. It provides access to the complete TypeScript codebase (700k lines) including the command system, terminal UI components built with React + Ink, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) implementation. The original npm package was quickly removed, but this recovery preserves the code structure for analysis and study of the CLI's architecture.

jarmuine/claude-code.blurb: Claude Code is a TypeScript CLI tool that allows developers to interact with Claude AI directly from the terminal for software engineering tasks like editing files, running commands, and searching codebases. It provides an agentic development environment with a React-based terminal UI, multi-agent coordination, and extensive tool integrations for automating coding workflows. This repository contains source code that was accidentally exposed through npm source maps in March 2026, representing a significant supply chain security incident.

0xSero/turboquant.description: TurboQuant: Near-optimal KV cache quantization for LLM inference (3-bit keys, 2-bit values) with Triton kernels + vLLM integration
0xSero/turboquant.blurb: TurboQuant is a KV cache compression library for LLM inference that quantizes keys to 3-bits and values to 2-bits using custom Triton kernels. It integrates with vLLM to reduce memory usage by 77% on dense transformers while maintaining throughput and generation quality. The implementation achieves 2x token capacity increases and modest speed improvements across RTX 3090/5090 GPUs.

motiful/cc-gateway.description: AI API identity gateway — reverse proxy that normalizes device fingerprints and telemetry for privacy-preserving API proxying
motiful/cc-gateway.blurb: CC Gateway is a reverse proxy that sits between Claude Code and Anthropic's API to normalize device fingerprints and telemetry data. It rewrites 40+ environment dimensions, device IDs, and system metrics to present a single canonical identity, preventing Claude Code from tracking individual machines across your network. The gateway handles OAuth token management centrally so client machines never need to contact Anthropic's authentication servers directly.

pengchengneo/Claude-Code.blurb: This is a reverse-engineered TypeScript codebase of Anthropic's `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` CLI tool, reconstructed from npm package source maps. It reveals numerous hidden features that are feature-flagged or restricted to internal users, including an AI pet system (Buddy), persistent assistant mode (Kairos), cloud-based deep planning (Ultraplan), and multi-agent coordination capabilities. The project is unofficial and intended for research purposes only, containing 1,987 TypeScript files with functionality not available in the public release.

sindresorhus/awesome.description: 😎 Awesome lists about all kinds of interesting topics
sindresorhus/awesome.blurb: This is a curated meta-repository that catalogs and organizes hundreds of "awesome lists" covering programming languages, tools, frameworks, and technical topics. Developers use it as a centralized directory to discover high-quality resources, libraries, and learning materials across virtually any technology domain. With over 450k stars, it has become the de facto standard for community-curated resource lists on GitHub.

sanbuphy/learn-coding-agent.blurb: This is a research repository that analyzes the architecture of CLI coding agents, specifically focused on reverse-engineering Claude Code through publicly available information. It provides deep technical analysis including tool systems, permission flows, telemetry mechanisms, and internal features like "undercover mode" for hiding AI authorship. The project includes comprehensive multilingual documentation covering 40+ tools, progressive harness mechanisms, and undocumented features discovered through community research.

Kuberwastaken/claurst.description: Your favorite Terminal Coding Agent, now in Rust & a Breakdown of the Claude Code leak & discoveries
Kuberwastaken/claurst.blurb: Claurst is a Rust reimplementation of Claude Code, Anthropic's AI coding CLI terminal agent, created after the original source code was leaked via an npm sourcemap. It provides the same AI-powered coding assistance and terminal integration as Claude Code but written in idiomatic Rust rather than TypeScript. The project was built using clean-room engineering principles, with separate AI agents handling specification and implementation phases to avoid copyright issues.

codeany-ai/open-agent-sdk-typescript.description: Agent-SDK without CLI dependencies, as an alternative to claude-agent-sdk, completely open source
codeany-ai/open-agent-sdk-typescript.blurb: The Open Agent SDK for TypeScript is an alternative to claude-agent-sdk that runs agent workflows in-process without CLI dependencies. It enables developers to deploy AI agents in any environment including cloud, serverless, and CI/CD pipelines while supporting custom tools and MCP server integration. The SDK runs the full agent loop in-process, eliminating the need for subprocess management.

Leonxlnx/agentic-ai-prompt-research.description: Research into how agentic AI coding assistants work — reconstructed prompt patterns, agent coordination, and security classification
Leonxlnx/agentic-ai-prompt-research.blurb: This is a research repository that reverse-engineers the prompt architecture and agent coordination patterns used by modern AI coding assistants like Claude Code through behavioral analysis. Developers and AI researchers use it to understand how these systems dynamically assemble prompts, coordinate multiple specialized agents, and implement security mechanisms for autonomous tool execution. The project documents 20+ reconstructed prompt patterns ranging from core system prompts to specialized agents for verification, exploration, and multi-agent orchestration.

JackChen-me/open-multi-agent.description: Production-grade multi-agent orchestration framework. Model-agnostic, supports team collaboration, task scheduling, and inter-agent communication.
JackChen-me/open-multi-agent.blurb: Open Multi-Agent is a TypeScript framework for orchestrating teams of AI agents that collaborate on complex tasks through automatic task scheduling and inter-agent communication. It enables building specialized agent teams where different agents handle planning, implementation, and review while the framework manages dependencies and parallel execution automatically. The framework is model-agnostic, allowing mixed teams of Claude and GPT agents running in a single Node.js process without subprocess overhead.

Gitlawb/openclaude.description: Claude Code opened to any LLM — OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, and 200+ models via OpenAI-compatible API shim
Gitlawb/openclaude.blurb: OpenClaude is a fork of the leaked Claude Code source that adds support for any OpenAI-compatible LLM instead of just Claude. It allows developers to use Claude Code's full toolset (bash, file operations, agents, MCP) with models like GPT-4o, DeepSeek, Gemini, or local models through Ollama. The project originated from Claude Code source maps that were accidentally exposed via npm in March 2026.

therealXiaomanChu/ex-skill.description: 把前任蒸馏成 AI Skill，用ta的方式跟你说话。Inspired by colleague-skill（同事skill）.
therealXiaomanChu/ex-skill.blurb: Ex-skill is a Python tool that creates AI personas based on your ex-partner's communication patterns using chat logs, social media, and photos. It analyzes relationship data to generate a conversational AI that mimics their speech patterns, personality traits, and shared memories for personal reflection or emotional processing. The system supports multiple Chinese messaging platforms (WeChat, QQ) and includes version management for iterating on the AI persona over time.

Leonxlnx/claw-dev.blurb: Claw Dev is a local multi-provider coding assistant launcher that provides a unified interface for various AI providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, OpenRouter, Ollama, and others through a bundled terminal client. It allows developers to switch between different AI model providers at startup while maintaining a consistent tool experience, routing non-Anthropic providers through a local compatibility proxy. The tool is designed to avoid provider-specific wrappers and auth conflicts by presenting itself as a single unified interface regardless of the underlying model provider.

emdash-cms/emdash.blurb: EmDash is a full-stack TypeScript CMS built on Astro and Cloudflare that reimagines WordPress with modern serverless architecture. It provides WordPress-like extensibility and admin UX while solving fundamental security issues by running plugins in sandboxed Worker isolates with declared capability manifests. Content is stored as structured JSON (Portable Text) rather than HTML, decoupling content from presentation for multi-platform rendering.

VoltAgent/awesome-design-md.description: Collection of DESIGN.md files that capture design systems from popular websites. Drop one into your project and let coding agents build matching UI.
VoltAgent/awesome-design-md.blurb: VoltAgent/awesome-design-md is a curated collection of DESIGN.md files that capture the design systems of popular websites in a standardized markdown format. Developers can drop these files into their projects and use them with AI coding agents to generate UI components that match the visual style of sites like Claude, Cohere, or ElevenLabs. The DESIGN.md format follows Google Stitch specifications and includes detailed sections covering color palettes, typography, components, and layout principles that LLMs can easily parse.

DenisSergeevitch/repo-task-proof-loop.description: Spec driven skill with subagents spawning
DenisSergeevitch/repo-task-proof-loop.blurb: Repo Task Proof Loop is a Python workflow skill that implements a structured approach to coding tasks using AI subagents and a strict build-verify loop within a repository. It creates persistent task folders with evidence collection, spawns project-scoped Codex and Claude subagents for different phases (spec freezing, building, verification, fixing), and maintains all proof artifacts locally for resumability and auditing. The system supports adaptive fan-out for parallel work when tasks can be cleanly decomposed.

openyak/openyak.description: An open-source desktop AI agent that handles your documents, files, and daily workflows — locally, with any model.
openyak/openyak.blurb: OpenYak is an open-source desktop AI agent that processes documents and files entirely on your local machine without uploading data to external services. It provides access to 100+ AI models from 20+ providers (cloud or local via Ollama) and includes 20+ built-in tools for file management, document creation, data analysis, and workflow automation. The platform offers free usage with 1M tokens per week on free models and connects to 46+ services through built-in connectors or MCP integration.
